frameworks.name: Bayesian confirmation theory
frameworks.one_line: Quantitative framework for updating belief in a hypothesis given evidence.

core_question: How should differences between an original experiment and its replication be taken into account when updating belief in the original finding?
key_assumptions: Belief change should follow Bayesian updating rather than dichotomous replicated/failed verdicts.
Differences between replication and original are inevitable and informative, not nuisance.
key_findings: Existing discussions of replication differences lack a principled quantitative framework.
Bayesian confirmation theory supplies such a framework, though it does not fully resolve the problem.
interpretations: Applying Bayesian confirmation to replication differences yields new insights even if it is not a complete solution.
limitations_noted: Authors acknowledge the framework will not entirely solve the replication-difference problem.
